data.title: Merge commits subject to review for files with no conflicts or changes — only conflicted/changed files should be reviewed
data.description:
## What happened

In a host project, after merging origin/main into a worktree branch, the review gate subjected ALL files in the merge to review — including files that came from main with no conflicts and no local changes. These files were already reviewed when they were merged to main in prior sessions.

## Expected behavior

For merge commits, only files that had conflicts or were changed on the worktree branch should be subject to review. Files that are unchanged between the two parents (no conflicts, no local modifications) should be excluded from the review scope. The pre-commit-review-gate.sh already has MERGE_HEAD handling that filters out incoming-only files, but this filtering may not be working correctly for all cases.

## Evidence from host project

The pre-commit hook timed out on the test gate because too many files were being checked individually. All 829 tests passed, TS clean, lint clean. The hook reported 'failing open (commit allowed)' but husky exit code was 124 (timeout).

## Fix needed

1. Verify that pre-commit-review-gate.sh MERGE_HEAD filtering correctly excludes files that have no conflicts and no local changes
2. Ensure the review scope for merge commits is limited to: conflicted files + files modified on the worktree branch
